prompt: Invent an original, vivid metaphor for the experience of learning a new language as an adult. Explain it in three sentences.
response: Learning a new language as an adult is like renovating a house you're already living in—you can't tear down the walls of your native tongue, so you build the new rooms awkwardly alongside them, tripping over furniture that used to make sense. For months, you live in the dust and scaffolding, speaking in half-finished sentences that echo strangely against unpainted walls. Then one morning you pour coffee in the new kitchen without thinking, and realize the house has quietly become yours.
